# @zakkster/lite-headless/carousel

Headless carousel per WAI-ARIA APG carousel pattern (basic with manual +
autoplay variants).

## Quick example

```js
import { createCarousel } from "@zakkster/lite-headless/carousel";

const carousel = createCarousel({
    autoplay: 4000,
    loop: true,
    onIndexChange: (i, reason) => console.log("now", i, "via", reason),
});

carousel.attachRoot(rootEl, { label: "Featured products" });
carousel.attachViewport(viewportEl);
slidesEls.forEach((el, i) => carousel.attachSlide(el, i));
carousel.attachPrev(prevBtn);
carousel.attachNext(nextBtn);
indicatorsEls.forEach((el, i) => carousel.attachIndicator(el, i));
carousel.attachPlayPause(playBtn);
```

## Options

| Option                 | Default        | Notes                                          |
| ---------------------- | -------------- | ---------------------------------------------- |
| `orientation`          | `"horizontal"` | `"horizontal"` or `"vertical"`                 |
| `autoplay`             | `null`         | ms interval; `null` = no autoplay              |
| `autoplayBehavior`     | `"pause"`      | `"pause"` or `"resume"` on hover/focus out     |
| `loop`                 | `false`        | wrap at edges                                  |
| `defaultIndex`         | `0`            | initial slide                                  |
| `uniformSlideWidth`    | `false`        | opt-in fast path (scrollLeft math vs IO)       |
| `respectReducedMotion` | `true`         | auto-disable autoplay if user prefers          |
| `scrollBehavior`       | `"smooth"`     | passed to scrollIntoView/scrollTo              |
| `onIndexChange`        | -              | `(index, reason)` callback                     |
| `onPlayingChange`      | -              | `(playing, reason)` callback                   |

`reason` strings: `"set"`, `"go"`, `"scroll"`, `"keyboard"`, `"user-nav"`,
`"user-toggle"`, `"autoplay-end"`, `"attribute"`.

## Source of truth

The current slide index is derived from one of two sources:

- **Default (correctness)**: `IntersectionObserver` reports
  `intersectionRatio` per slide; the slide with the highest ratio wins.
  Handles uneven slide widths, partial scroll positions, and any
  scroll-snap layout.
- **Opt-in fast path** (`uniformSlideWidth: true`): rAF-throttled
  `scrollLeft / slideWidth` math. Single passive scroll listener, no
  observer overhead, no allocation per frame. Use when all slides are
  the same width.

The imperative API (`go`/`next`/`prev`) commits the index optimistically
before scroll settles, so consumers see the new index immediately after
their call. The scroll-driven path then re-confirms (no-op if matching).

## Layout

The primitive is layout-agnostic. Provide the viewport with:

```css
.viewport {
    overflow: auto;
    scroll-snap-type: x mandatory;
    display: flex;
}
.viewport > .slide {
    scroll-snap-align: start;
    flex: 0 0 100%;
}
```

For vertical: `scroll-snap-type: y mandatory` and `flex-direction: column`.

## Attachment methods

| Method                          | Returns         | Notes                              |
| ------------------------------- | --------------- | ---------------------------------- |
| `attachRoot(el, { label? })`    | cleanup `()`    | role="region", aria-roledescription="carousel" |
| `attachViewport(el)`            | cleanup `()`    | scrollable container; keyboard nav |
| `attachSlide(el, idx, opts?)`   | cleanup `()`    | `opts: { label }`                  |
| `attachPrev(el)` / `attachNext` | cleanup `()`    | button; auto aria-label + aria-controls |
| `attachIndicator(el, idx)`      | cleanup `()`    | role="tab"; tabindex roving        |
| `attachPlayPause(el)`           | cleanup `()`    | toggle; aria-pressed reactive      |

## Imperative API

```js
carousel.go(i, behavior?)           // behavior: "smooth" | "auto"
carousel.next() / prev()
carousel.first() / last()
carousel.play() / pause() / toggle()
carousel.isPlaying()                // boolean
carousel.index()             // number
carousel.slideCount()               // number
carousel.destroy()                  // idempotent
```

Reactive accessors (return signal getters; subscribe inside an `effect`):

```js
carousel.index()                    // signal -> current index
carousel.playing()                  // signal -> is autoplay running
```

## Keyboard (when viewport has focus)

- `ArrowLeft` / `ArrowRight` (horizontal) — prev / next
- `ArrowUp` / `ArrowDown` (vertical) — prev / next
- `Home` / `End` — first / last
- Any keyboard nav pauses autoplay (sticky until user resumes)

Indicator tablist: each indicator has `tabindex="0"` when current,
`tabindex="-1"` otherwise (roving tabindex pattern). Consumers can also
add arrow-key handling on the tablist for tab-to-tab navigation.

## Autoplay behavior

Per WAI-ARIA APG recommendation:

- Pause on hover (if `autoplayBehavior: "pause"`)
- Pause on focus-within
- Pause on any user navigation (prev/next/indicator/keyboard)
- Resume on hover-out **only if** the user hasn't manually paused
- Reduced-motion users: autoplay off by default
  (override with `respectReducedMotion: false`)
- At end-of-list (no loop): autoplay stops with reason
  `"autoplay-end"`

## ARIA painted attributes

| Element       | Attributes                                       |
| ------------- | ------------------------------------------------ |
| Root          | `role="region"`, `aria-roledescription="carousel"`, `aria-label`, `data-orientation` |
| Viewport      | `aria-live="off"` while playing, `"polite"` while paused/manual |
| Slide         | `role="group"`, `aria-roledescription="slide"`, `aria-label="N of M"`, `data-active (boolean)` |
| Prev / Next   | `aria-label`, `aria-controls` (viewport id)      |
| Indicator     | `role="tab"`, `aria-selected`, `aria-controls`, `tabindex`, `data-active` |
| Play/Pause    | `aria-pressed`, `aria-label`, `data-playing (boolean)` |

## Element wrapper

```html
<lite-carousel label="Featured" autoplay="4000" loop>
    <button data-carousel-prev>←</button>
    <div data-carousel-viewport tabindex="0">
        <div data-carousel-slide>Slide 1</div>
        <div data-carousel-slide>Slide 2</div>
        <div data-carousel-slide>Slide 3</div>
    </div>
    <button data-carousel-next>→</button>
    <div role="tablist">
        <button data-carousel-indicator>●</button>
        <button data-carousel-indicator>○</button>
        <button data-carousel-indicator>○</button>
    </div>
    <button data-carousel-play-pause>⏯</button>
</lite-carousel>
```

Slides and indicators auto-derive their index from sibling order, OR
the consumer can specify `data-index="N"` for explicit numbering.

Reactive attributes:

- `index` — current slide (two-way; v0.7.12 cascade guard applied)

Read-once attributes:

- `label`, `orientation`, `autoplay`, `loop`, `uniform-slide-width`

Dispatched events:

- `indexchange` — `{ detail: { index, reason } }`
- `playingchange` — `{ detail: { playing, reason } }`

## CSS contract

This primitive's painted attributes follow the canonical CSS contract
in `docs/CSS_CONTRACT.md`. See that doc for the cross-primitive
taxonomy (booleans like `data-open` / `data-disabled` / `data-loading`,
enums like `data-status` / `data-side`, ARIA pairings, host accessor
naming, and event naming).
